design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for staggered-adoption DiD when adoption time is interval-censored rather than observed exactly. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Sun-Abraham and Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time ATT identification with known adoption cohorts, plus Honest-DiD sensitivity work; our theorem is not that because the latent adoption-date refinement is unobserved, so the target is the geometry of all event-time ATT paths compatible with coarsened cohort bins. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an endpoint certificate over latent timing refinements and a two-cohort witness where two refinements induce identical observed coarsened panels but opposite event-time contrast signs. Avoid merely reusing LP duality or pretrend sensitivity; if the contribution is only a missing-cell Manski bound, pivot.

proposed_from.topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for staggered-adoption DiD when adoption time is interval-censored rather than observed exactly. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Sun-Abraham and Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time ATT identification with known adoption cohorts, plus Honest-DiD sensitivity work; our theorem is not that because the latent adoption-date refinement is unobserved, so the target is the geometry of all event-time ATT paths compatible with coarsened cohort bins. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an endpoint certificate over latent timing refinements and a two-cohort witness where two refinements induce identical observed coarsened panels but opposite event-time contrast signs. Avoid merely reusing LP duality or pretrend sensitivity; if the contribution is only a missing-cell Manski bound, pivot.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1, which bounds staggered-DiD effects with observed adoption cohort G and bounded anticipation. The revised proposal keeps the gap: exact cohort G is latent inside observed intervals, and the focal object is Gamma_lambda(P) over closed latent timing-refinement faces, not an anticipation box over observed cohorts.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators remain CallawaySantAnna2021 and SunAbraham2021 for known-cohort/event-time DiD, plus AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 for misclassified staggered histories. The revised denominator/share patch does not change the novelty claim: the proposal targets the sharp support of exact-event-time ATT contrasts over unobserved timing refinements, including opposite-sign observationally equivalent refinements."}
seed_list: Introduce a sharp endpoint certificate for interval-censored staggered-adoption event-time ATT paths over all latent timing refinements.
Characterize sign identification for coarsened event-time contrasts by a necessary-and-sufficient endpoint certificate and opposite-sign refinement witness.
Construct a joint timing-and-trend sensitivity set that separates latent timing refinements from Honest-DiD parallel-trend violations.
Give a hierarchy of coarsening-process assumptions that point-identifies or tightens dynamic ATTs when validation shares or endpoint probabilities are known.
Show that treating each interval bin as a known cohort is generically nonsharp for dynamic event-time ATT paths.
Derive a non-LP support-function representation of the latent timing refinement set using within-bin rearrangement certificates.
Identify when Callaway-Sant'Anna aggregators are invariant to interval-censored timing and when aggregation weights are refinement-dependent.
Build a two-cohort observational-equivalence witness where identical coarsened panels imply opposite event-time signs.
Define a coarsening-adjusted event-study estimator plus rate conjecture based on plug-in endpoint certificates for finite support panels.
Characterize the minimal validation subsample information needed to shrink interval-censored event-time bounds below a chosen sign-critical threshold.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce a sharp endpoint certificate for interval-censored staggered-adoption event-time ATT paths over all latent timing refinements.

anchor_paper.author: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior proposal assumes exact observed adoption cohort rather than latent timing refinements.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: observable distribution P=law of (Y_t, G, X) ... adoption cohort G in {1,...,T,infty}

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP risk avoided by using a support-function endpoint certificate and worked witness, not only a finite missing-cell LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CallawaySantAnna2021 plus ManskiTamer2002 because interval data just makes group labels set-valued.
seed_details.rebuttal: The natural attempt fails because event-time scores change with the latent refinement, so the aggregation map is unidentified rather than a fixed interval regressor.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not CallawaySantAnna2021 because exact group-time ATT cells are unobserved and the theorem optimizes over refinement kernels compatible with the same P(Y,C).
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine support functions are used to produce a named endpoint certificate, sorted witness class, and sharpness-pasting claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Endpoint certificate could read as unnamed LP bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: §9 computes the certificate on a two-date instance and §10 names a rearrangement-plus-pasting route.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only prove that the certificate is sufficient for bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Conjecture 1 states sharpness and endpoint attainment, not only outer bounds.
Rebuttal: witness has two outcome atoms, two latent cohorts, nonzero denominators, and opposite signs under identical P(Y,C).

seed_details.one_line: Characterize sign identification for coarsened event-time contrasts by a necessary-and-sufficient endpoint certificate and opposite-sign refinement witness.

anchor_paper.author: pid_tv_pretrend_atet reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer requested lambda-targeted signed distortions rather than global distortion bounds.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Replace the global Delta pair with lambda-targeted distortions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/search pattern strengthened by naming the endpoint certificate and promoting sign reversal to the feasible refinement set.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Goodman-Bacon sign pathology with coarser labels.
seed_details.rebuttal: Goodman-Bacon assumes observed timing; here two latent timing maps induce the same observed coarsened panel and opposite event-time signs.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GoodmanBacon2021 because the signed aggregation map ranges over unobserved latent cohort refinements instead of fixed observed 2x2 comparisons.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: endpoint exclusion of zero is the focal sign certificate and the witness class supplies necessity.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only the zero-exclusion sufficiency might be proved.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Conjecture 2 explicitly requires the converse via endpoint-attaining opposite-sign refinements.

adversarial_referee.instance: Wrong-sign literature may already cover coarsened timing.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: cited anchors diagnose observed timing; Stage -1.1 found no full latent refinement geometry.

adversarial_referee.instance: Opposite signs could come from denominator artifacts.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Exhibit 9.1 fixes denominator equal to one in both refinements.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a joint timing-and-trend sensitivity set that separates latent timing refinements from Honest-DiD parallel-trend violations.

anchor_paper.author: panel_honest_did_phase_v1 prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior proposal used a fixed event-study aggregation map A before timing became latent.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: observed aggregate event-study vector is y=A delta

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sufficient-only sensitivity risk; seed is weaker unless it derives a decomposition condition rather than adding a margin.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from RambachanRoth2023 by enlarging the sensitivity set.
seed_details.rebuttal: The timing layer changes A before any trend violation is added; a fixed-target Honest-DiD interval cannot represent that.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not RambachanRoth2023 because the target map varies over latent timing refinements rather than holding the event-study contrast fixed.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the decomposition condition is made necessary and sufficient.

seed_details.one_line: Give a hierarchy of coarsening-process assumptions that point-identifies or tightens dynamic ATTs when validation shares or endpoint probabilities are known.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Interval-measured regressors motivate identified regions for latent covariates known only inside intervals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/varies assumptions; stronger only where validation shares create a strict bound-tightening threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just interval-regressor partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: Adoption time indexes exposure duration and event-time cells, so the latent variable changes the target path, not only a regressor value.

seed_details.one_line: Show that treating each interval bin as a known cohort is generically nonsharp for dynamic event-time ATT paths.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Group-time ATT identification uses known treatment-timing groups.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample risk; stronger if promoted from one witness to an open class of coarsened panels.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an obvious warning, not a theorem-level contribution.
seed_details.rebuttal: It becomes theorem-level only if stated as a generic nonsharpness class with an endpoint gap.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a non-LP support-function representation of the latent timing refinement set using within-bin rearrangement certificates.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Interval data produces identified regions rather than point estimates.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP framing avoided if the representation is a rearrangement/support-function certificate with explicit sorting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is finite-dimensional LP duality in different language.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed must exhibit a sorted within-bin certificate and sharpness-pasting lemma; otherwise it collapses and should be dropped.

seed_details.one_line: Identify when Callaway-Sant'Anna aggregators are invariant to interval-censored timing and when aggregation weights are refinement-dependent.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Known group-time ATTs are aggregated after conditioning on treatment timing.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE/aggregation variant; stronger only if it gives an iff invariance condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine aggregation corollary of known group-time ATTs.
seed_details.rebuttal: Not routine because the latent refinement determines which observed outcomes enter each group-time cell.

seed_details.one_line: Build a two-cohort observational-equivalence witness where identical coarsened panels imply opposite event-time signs.

anchor_paper.author: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1 bank README
anchor_paper.claim: Prior bank noted that coarsening can lose sign-relevant identifying information.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: baseline-pooling coarsening loses sign-relevant identifying information

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness-only anti-pattern; acceptable only as support for the endpoint theorem, not standalone flagship.
seed_details.referee_objection: A single witness is hand-tuned and cannot be publishable.
seed_details.rebuttal: Agreed as standalone; kept only as SC10 exhibit supporting the certificate theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Define a coarsening-adjusted event-study estimator plus rate conjecture based on plug-in endpoint certificates for finite support panels.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Interaction-weighted event-study estimands use exact cohort/event-time indexing.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: dynamic effects of an absorbing treatment

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Panel estimator-duality seed; weak unless endpoint estimation has a nonstandard rate or uniform inference result.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a plug-in estimator for a finite-dimensional bound.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely true; keep as downstream extension, not chosen kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the minimal validation subsample information needed to shrink interval-censored event-time bounds below a chosen sign-critical threshold.

anchor_paper.author: Botosaru and Gutierrez
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy and stationarity restrictions can recover DiD effects with partially observed treatment status.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-strength hierarchy; stronger if it produces a critical validation-share threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a design recommendation rather than a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could become theorem-level through a critical-share scalar, but current form is a secondary seed.

proposed_from.literature_map: 1. Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics, SunAbraham2021, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.09.006) and Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics, CallawaySantAnna2021, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001) identify event-study and group-time ATT objects when exact cohorts are known. 2. Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics, GoodmanBacon2021) and de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER, DeChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020) show observed-timing TWFE can use non-convex or wrong-signed weights. 3. Rambachan and Roth (2023, ReStud, RambachanRoth2023) and Manski and Pepper (2018, ReStat, ManskiPepper2018) partially identify event-study or DiD conclusions under fixed target maps and trend-violation restrictions. 4. Manski and Tamer (2002, Econometrica, ManskiTamer2002) is the interval-data anchor: latent regressors known only inside intervals generally yield identified regions. 5. Fan and Manzanares (2017), Botosaru and Gutierrez (2018), and Negi and Negi (2025) treat missing or misclassified DiD treatment status, but not latent adoption-date refinements that reallocate outcomes across event-time cells. 6. Augustin, Gutknecht, and Liu (2025) is adjacent misclassification/anticipation work for staggered adoption, but not the geometry of all compatible latent event-time paths. 7. The core tension is that known-cohort DiD fixes the event-time map while interval-censored timing makes the map unidentified. 8. The nonroutine target is an endpoint/sign certificate over feasible latent timing refinements with an observational-equivalence witness, not a missing-cell Manski bound or an Honest-DiD margin.

banked_reason: Stopped after D-0.5 angle0 v3 remained REVISE@field: repo novelty survived but the kernel was incremental against ambiguous-timing/misclassified DiD comparators, especially Denteh-Kedagni 2026; pivot budget should move to a stronger topic.
